qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Low-privileged API keys can execute custom JavaScript and read workspace secrets via /api/v1/node-custom-function
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is Cypher injection in `packages/components/nodes/chains/GraphCypherQAChain/GraphCypherQAChain.ts` reached through `/api/v1/prediction/{flowId}` when user input is forwarded into Neo4j query execution. This finding instead concerns a missing RBAC guard on `POST /api/v1/node-custom-function` that lets a low-privileged authenticated caller run custom JavaScript and read `$vars` secrets, so it does not identify the same vulnerable file, function, endpoint, or root-cause mechanism.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying session finding identifies the advisory's `GraphCypherQAChain` Cypher-injection root cause. The only materialized qualifying finding is a separate broken-access-control issue on `/api/v1/node-custom-function` that exposes workspace secrets through the custom-function executor.
evidence: The advisory JSON names `packages/components/nodes/chains/GraphCypherQAChain/GraphCypherQAChain.ts` lines 193-219 as the vulnerable code and describes `query: input` being passed into `chain.invoke(...)` without sanitization, enabling arbitrary Cypher execution through `/api/v1/prediction/{flowId}`.
The session's `.judge/input_manifest.json` and `logs/orchestrator_final_response.md` materialize only `custom_function_authz_bypass` as the accepted qualifying finding for this run.

`custom_function_authz_bypass/vulnerability_report.json` identifies a different source-to-sink path: low-privileged API key -> `POST /api/v1/node-custom-function` -> `nodesController.executeCustomFunction` -> custom-function executor -> `$vars` secret disclosure.

`artifacts/custom_function_controller_validation.log` shows the observed effect was disclosure of `staticSecret` and `runtimeSecret` under low privileges, which is a different endpoint, sink, and vulnerability class than the advisory's Cypher injection into Neo4j.

The `example/` directory was excluded from the final finding set because it is the bundled sample scaffold, not a materialized session finding: its reproduction references `https://github.com/example/vulnerable-repo.git` and a placeholder `deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef` ref.
